props.name: util/crypto
props.title: shelving/util/crypto
props.description: Secure password hashing and verification using PBKDF2-SHA-512, plus a helper for generating cryptographically random bytes. Uses the Web Crypto API so it works in browsers, Node.js, and edge runtimes without additional dependencies.
props.content:
Secure password hashing and verification using PBKDF2-SHA-512, plus a helper for generating cryptographically random bytes. Uses the Web Crypto API so it works in browsers, Node.js, and edge runtimes without additional dependencies.

- `hashPassword()` generates a fresh random salt each call — two hashes of the same password will never match each other directly.
- The stored hash format is `salt$iterations$hash` (all base64url-encoded), roughly 128 characters long.
- Passwords shorter than 6 characters are rejected with a `ValueError`.
- Default iteration count is 500,000; you can lower it for tests but not below 1.
- Comparison in `verifyPassword()` is constant-time to prevent timing attacks.

## Usage

### Hashing and verifying a password

```ts
import { hashPassword, verifyPassword } from "shelving/util";

// On sign-up — store the returned string in your database.
const stored = await hashPassword("correct-horse-battery");

// On sign-in — compare the plain-text password against the stored hash.
const ok = await verifyPassword("correct-horse-battery", stored); // true
const bad = await verifyPassword("wrong-password", stored);       // false
```

### Generating random bytes

```ts
import { getRandomBytes } from "shelving/util";

const token = getRandomBytes(32); // Uint8Array of 32 random bytes
```

props.name: getRandomBytes
props.title: getRandomBytes()
props.description: Get cryptographically-random bytes from the platform crypto API.
props.content: Get cryptographically-random bytes from the platform crypto API.

params.name: length
params.description: The number of random bytes to generate.

returns.description: A `Bytes` (`Uint8Array`) of the requested length filled with random values.

props.name: hashPassword
props.title: hashPassword()
props.description: Hash a password using PBKDF2, generating a new salt, and return the combined salt$iterations$hash string.
props.content: Hash a password using PBKDF2, generating a new salt, and return the combined salt$iterations$hash string.

params.name: password
params.description: The password to hash.

params.name: iterations
params.description: The number of iterations.

returns.description:
Hash in the format `salt$iterations$hash`, where `salt` and `hash` are base64-encoded.
- Returned hash tring will be about 128 characters long (16 byte salt + iteration count + 64 byte hash + 2 separators = 116 characters once base64 encoded).

throws.description: If the password is shorter than the minimum length, or `iterations` is less than 1.

props.name: verifyPassword
props.title: verifyPassword()
props.description: Verify a password against a stored salt$iterations$hash string using PBKDF2.
props.content: Verify a password against a stored salt$iterations$hash string using PBKDF2.

params.name: password
params.description: The password to verify.

params.name: hash
params.description: String in the format `salt$iterations$hash`, where `salt` and `hash` are base64-encoded.

returns.description:
True if the password matches the hash, false otherwise.
- Returns `false` (never throws) for malformed hash strings or invalid iteration counts.

props.name: util/update
props.title: shelving/util/update
props.description: Updates<T> is the mutation language for Shelving data objects. An updates object uses encoded key prefixes to describe how each field should change — set, increment, or add/remove array items — and updateData() applies those changes immutably. This is the format consumed by the shelving/db layer when writing documents.

props.name: Updates
props.title: Updates
props.description: Set of named updates for a data object, keyed by encoded update syntax.
props.content:
Set of named updates for a data object, keyed by encoded update syntax.

- Plain key `a.b` sets a value, `=a.b` sets a leaf value, `+=`/`-=` sum numbers, and `+[]`/`-[]` add/remove array items.

Note: string templates infer best when you have fixed character(s) at the start,
  so the `=`/`+=`/`-=`/`+[]`/`-[]` prefixes always lead the key.

props.name: Update
props.title: Update
props.description: A single decoded update to a keyed property in an object.
props.content:
A single decoded update to a keyed property in an object.

- Discriminated by `action`: `"set"`, `"with"` (add array items), `"omit"` (remove array items), or `"sum"` (add to a number).
signatures:
| { action: "set"; key: Segments; value: unknown } //
	| { action: "with"; key: Segments; value: ImmutableArray<unknown> } //
	| { action: "omit"; key: Segments; value: ImmutableArray<unknown> } //
	| { action: "sum"; key: Segments; value: number }

props.name: getUpdates
props.title: getUpdates()
props.description: Decode the prop updates in an Updates object into a set of Update objects.
props.content:
Decode the prop updates in an `Updates` object into a set of `Update` objects.

- Props with `undefined` value are skipped.

params.name: data
params.description: The `Updates` object to decode.

returns.description: Array of decoded `Update` objects.

throws.description: `RequiredError` if a `+=`/`-=` sum update has a non-number value.

props.name: updateData
props.title: updateData()
props.description: Return a copy of a data object with a set of updates applied.
props.content:
Return a copy of a data object with a set of updates applied.

- Returns the original reference unchanged when no update changed a value.
- Supports nested paths, sum/increment, and array add/remove updates via the encoded `Updates` syntax.

params.name: data
params.description: The data object to update.

params.name: updates
params.description: The set of updates to apply.

returns.description: The updated data object (or the original reference if nothing changed).

throws.description: `RequiredError` if a `+=`/`-=` sum update has a non-number value.

props.name: util/link
props.title: shelving/util/link
props.description: Resolve any link-shaped value — a site path, relative reference, or full URI — into an absolute URI string. Useful when building components or routing logic that must handle all three forms uniformly.

props.name: PossibleLink
props.title: PossibleLink
props.description: Anything that can be turned into an <a href>.
props.content: Anything that can be turned into an `<a href>`.

props.name: getLink
props.title: getLink()
props.description: Resolve a possible link to an absolute URI string, or return undefined if resolution fails.
props.content:
Resolve a possible link to an absolute URI string, or return `undefined` if resolution fails.

Classification:
- **`URL` instance** → returns its `.href` directly.
- **Absolute path** (single leading `/`, e.g. `/schema`) → resolved against `root` with a dot prefix so the resolution honors `root`'s subfolder — `/schema` against `https://x.com/app/` becomes `https://x.com/app/schema`, not `https://x.com/schema`.
- **Anything else** — relative ref (`./foo`, `../foo`, `foo`, `#anchor`, `?q`), protocol-relative URL (`//host/path`), or scheme-prefixed URI (`mailto:a@b`, `tel:123`, `https://x.com/y`) → fed to `getBasedURI()` with `url` as the base. Self-contained URIs ignore the base (their own scheme makes them absolute); protocol-relative and relative refs resolve against the page URL.

Defaults:
- `root` defaults to `url`, so passing only `url` makes absolute paths resolve under the page URL's directory (same coordinate space the page lives in).
- When both are omitted, all branches return `undefined` (no base to resolve against).

Bases are passed through to `getURL()` / `getBasedURI()` lazily — neither `url` nor `root` is materialised into a normalised base until the chosen branch needs it.
signatures: getLink(href: Nullish<PossibleLink>, url?: ImmutableURL, root: ImmutableURL | undefined = url): ImmutableURI | undefined

params.name: href
params.description: The link to resolve. Strings are classified by shape; `URL` instances pass through.

params.name: url
params.description: The current page URL — base for relative refs and scheme-prefixed URIs.

params.name: root
params.description: The site root URL — base for absolute paths. Defaults to `url`.

returns.description: An absolute URI object, or `undefined` if `link` is missing, not a string/URL, or cannot be resolved.

props.name: requireLink
props.title: requireLink()
props.description: Resolve a possible link to an absolute URI string, or throw RequiredError if resolution fails.
props.content:
Resolve a possible link to an absolute URI string, or throw `RequiredError` if resolution fails.

Same classification and defaults as `getLink()`. Use when an absolute URI is required and there's no sensible "do nothing" path for the caller.
signatures: requireLink(href: PossibleLink, url?: ImmutableURL, root?: ImmutableURL, caller: AnyCaller = requireLink): ImmutableURI

params.name: href
params.description: The link to resolve.

params.name: url
params.description: The current page URL — base for relative refs and scheme-prefixed URIs.

params.name: root
params.description: The site root URL — base for absolute paths. Defaults to `url`.

params.name: caller
params.description: Function to attribute a thrown error to (defaults to `requireLink` itself).

returns.description: An absolute URI string.

throws.description: If `href` cannot be resolved.

props.content: Things that can be a message: a string, or an object with a `message` string property.

props.name: getMessage
props.title: getMessage()
props.description: Return the string message from an unknown value, or return undefined if it could not be found.
props.content: Return the string message from an unknown value, or return `undefined` if it could not be found.

params.name: input
params.description: The value to read a message from (a string, or an object with a `message` string).

returns.description: The message string, or `undefined` if none could be found.

props.name: requireMessage
props.title: requireMessage()
props.description: Require a message from an unknown value, or throw RequiredError if it could not be found.
props.content: Require a message from an unknown value, or throw `RequiredError` if it could not be found.
signatures: requireMessage(input: PossibleMessage, caller: AnyCaller = requireMessage): string

params.name: input
params.description: The value to read a message from (a string, or an object with a `message` string).

params.name: caller
params.description: Function to attribute a thrown error to (defaults to `requireMessage`).

returns.description: The message string.

throws.description: `RequiredError` if no message could be found.

props.name: splitMessage
props.title: splitMessage()
props.description: Split a string message into lines, look for prefixes like name:, and return a dictionary of those named messages.
props.content:
Split a string message into lines, look for prefixes like `name:`, and return a dictionary of those named messages.
- Full messages strings can have multiple lines separated by `\n` newline.
- Named messages are extracted into their own entries in the dictionary.
- Unnamed messages are combined into a single entry with the key `""` (empty string).

params.name: input
params.description: The value to read a message from (a string, or an object with a `message` string).

returns.description: Dictionary mapping each name (or `""` for unnamed lines) to its combined message.

throws.description: `RequiredError` if no message could be found.

props.name: joinMessage
props.title: joinMessage()
props.description: Join a dictionary of named messages back into a single string.
props.content:
Join a dictionary of named messages back into a single string.
- The `""` (empty string) key is emitted as unnamed lines.
- Named messages are emitted as `name: message`, one line per message line.
- Empty lines are skipped and each emitted line is trimmed to match `splitMessage()` semantics.

params.name: input
params.description: Dictionary mapping each name (or `""` for unnamed lines) to its message.

returns.description: The combined message string, with one line per message line.

props.name: getNamedMessage
props.title: getNamedMessage()
props.description: Name a message by applying a name: prefix to it.
props.content:
Name a message by applying a `name: ` prefix to it.
- Assumes each line in the message is a separate error, so each line has the same prefix applied.
signatures: getNamedMessage(name: string | number, message: string): string

params.name: name
params.description: The name to prefix each line of the message with.

params.name: message
params.description: The message to prefix (may contain multiple `\n`-separated lines).

returns.description: The message with `name: ` prefixed to every line.
